content:
# {{projectName}} - MariaDB Database Project

This is a MariaDB database project created using the CLI tool.

## Project Structure

```
{{projectName}}/
├── data/           # Persistent data storage
├── migrations/     # Database migrations
├── seeds/         # Seed data
├── config/        # Configuration files
├── scripts/       # Utility scripts
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .env
└── README.md
```

## Getting Started

1. Make sure you have Docker and Docker Compose installed on your system.
2. Review and modify the `.env` file with your desired settings.
3. Start the database:
   ```bash
   docker compose up -d
   ```
4. The MariaDB server will be available at `localhost:3306`

## Default Credentials

- Root User: root
- Root Password: (set in .env)
- Database User: (set in .env)
- Database Password: (set in .env)
- Database Name: {{projectName}}

## Connecting to the Database

You can connect to the database using any MariaDB client with the following connection details:

- Host: localhost
- Port: 3306
- User: (set in .env)
- Password: (set in .env)
- Database: {{projectName}}

## Database Migrations

Place your SQL migration files in the `migrations/` directory. Files will be executed in alphabetical order when the container is first created.

## Seed Data

Place your seed data SQL files in the `seeds/` directory. These will be executed after migrations.

## Data Persistence

All database data is stored in the `data/` directory and will persist between container restarts.

## Stopping the Database

To stop the database:

```bash
docker compose down
```

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container logs:
   ```bash
   docker compose logs mariadb
   ```
2. Access container shell:
   ```bash
   docker compose exec mariadb bash
   ```
3. Connect to MariaDB CLI:
   ```bash
   docker compose exec mariadb mariadb -u root -p
   ```
